title: rubus fluvius
intro: Rubus fluvius, also known as river raspberry or salmonberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high content of vitamin C, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Rubus fluvius include reducing inflammation, protecting against oxidative stress, and supporting cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine and as a food ingredient in jams, jellies, and other culinary applications.
